response.description_long:
Clinicore automates the seam where specialty clinics lose the most administrative time: prior authorization, which the site calls the top burden in specialty care. The failure it targets is specific — staff hours on payer portals and phone calls, patients waiting days, and denials for documentation that was sitting in the chart all along.

The product runs order to decision. Integrated with Epic, Cerner, and athenahealth, Clinicore watches the order feed and screens each order against payer and plan; orders that need no authorization are marked cleared, which the company argues is half the value. For the rest, a rules engine maps the payer's policy to a checklist of required evidence — diagnosis codes, staging, labs, imaging reports, prior therapies — pulls each item from the chart, and assembles the payer-specific packet, flagging anything missing before submission. Packets go out electronically where payers support it, and Clinicore's operations team handles portal and fax submission where they do not. Status flows back into the EHR, so staff work one queue instead of a dozen portals.

The automation is deliberately hybrid: a dedicated policy team keeps payer rules current, and a clinical operations team reviews edge cases before anything goes out. Denials feed an appeals pipeline that assembles the appeal from the same evidence and attaches the payer's own policy citation, with denial analytics by payer, code, and policy.

The claimed results are concrete: authorizations submitted in minutes instead of hours, first-pass approval rates above 90 percent, and staff time on authorizations cut by more than half. A 12-site oncology group is described as moving its backlog from three days to same-day within two months, and an imaging network cut authorization-related reschedules by two thirds.

Customers are specialty practices and clinic groups in oncology, cardiology, rheumatology, and imaging, from single sites to groups with 40 locations, with specialty-specific workflows like urgent-start oncology and step-therapy documentation for biologics. Go-live runs four to six weeks. Pricing is per provider per month with volume pricing for groups, no per-transaction fees, and no charge for appeals. Clinicore is HIPAA compliant, signs a BAA with every customer, and is SOC 2 Type II certified.

The company was founded in 2020 in Nashville, TN by Carmen Villalobos, a practice administrator who ran operations for a multi-site cardiology group, and health-data engineers David Lin and Marcus Webb. It is 60 people and remote-friendly, with clinical operations concentrated in Nashville — a team mix of engineers, former practice staff, and nurses who now encode payer policy instead of fighting it.
